title: DeepSeek Service Restored After 13-Hour Outage
detail: DeepSeek's consumer chatbot platform was fully restored at approximately 10:33 AM CST on March 30, following a two-incident outage spanning more than 13 hours. The outage affected all DeepSeek API and consumer web services. DeepSeek did not issue a formal post-mortem or public explanation. The incident drew attention to the infrastructure gap between Chinese AI consumer deployments and US counterparts: OpenAI and Anthropic have maintained far higher uptime despite similarly large user bases. Industry observers noted that DeepSeek, founded as a quantitative hedge fund AI lab rather than a product company, may lack the infrastructure engineering depth needed for consumer-grade availability guarantees. The episode highlighted that hardware restrictions are not the only asymmetry between US and Chinese AI labs — organizational maturity and operational reliability remain significant differentiators.

title: SMIC Reports Record 2025 Revenue of $9.33B; Releases Aggressive 2026 Action Plan
detail: Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC) released its 2025 annual results and 2026 action plan. Revenue reached $9.327 billion for full-year 2025 — up approximately 20.9% year-over-year and a new record — while net profit grew 39% to $685 million and gross margin improved to 21%. The 2026 capital expenditure plan was held flat at approximately $8.1 billion, focused on legacy-node capacity expansion in BCD process, analog circuits, MCUs, and display driver ICs where SMIC faces less international competition. SMIC targets 'above-industry' revenue growth for 2026. The results arrive amid US government allegations that SMIC supplied chipmaking equipment to Iran's military — allegations SMIC has not addressed publicly. TrendForce projects China's domestic share of its AI accelerator chip market will reach 50% in 2026, driven by SMIC-manufactured Huawei Ascend chips displacing Nvidia hardware.

title: ByteDance and Alibaba Confirm Plans to Order Huawei Ascend 950PR AI Chips
detail: Following a Reuters report initially published March 27, ByteDance and Alibaba confirmed plans to place substantial orders for Huawei's new Ascend 950PR AI inference chip after successful internal testing. The 950PR represents a significant advance over the Ascend 910C: it is optimized for inference workloads and improves CUDA ecosystem compatibility — removing the main adoption barrier that had previously limited Huawei chip uptake at Chinese hyperscalers with existing CUDA-based model deployments. Huawei plans to ship approximately 750,000 Ascend 950PR units in 2026, with mass production beginning in April and volume shipments in H2 2026. Pricing is approximately 50,000 yuan (~$6,900) for the DDR memory version and 70,000 yuan (~$9,700) for the premium HBM version. The orders reflect a strategic acceleration of China's AI compute stack localization as US H20 chip restrictions (effective April 2025) eliminate remaining Nvidia options for Chinese buyers.
